alerts.title: US-Iran strike cycle continues unabated; ceasefire framework collapses on Day 122
alerts.summary: Iran and US exchanged multiple strikes June 28-29, with Iran targeting Ali Al-Salem Airbase (Kuwait) and 5th Fleet HQ (Bahrain); US retaliated against Iranian coastal missile/drone sites near Strait of Hormuz. Multiple sources (AP, CNBC, PBS, BBC, NBC, Al Jazeera, NYT) confirm ceasefire framework has effectively dissolved. Trump administration maintains hardline posture; formal talks remain on hold.
alerts.region: Persian Gulf, Kuwait, Bahrain, Strait of Hormuz

alerts.title: Iran nuclear breakout window remains 1-2 weeks amid active hostilities
alerts.summary: IAEA confirms Iran maintains 60% U-235 enrichment. UN nuclear chief states inspectors will visit Iranian nuclear sites only after final deal completion. Concurrent military escalation and collapsed diplomatic framework narrows window for de-escalation. Global threat level remains CRITICAL (9/10).

alerts.title: Israel-Hezbollah escalation accelerates after security framework rejection
alerts.summary: Hezbollah formally rejected US-brokered Israel-Lebanon security framework on June 28, calling it 'surrender.' Israel responded with strikes on Beirut and confirmed destruction of major Hezbollah tunnel in South Lebanon (33.96°N, 36.08°E). Netanyahu reiterated Israel's freedom to strike Hezbollah unilaterally. Framework collapse signals imminent escalation risk along Israel-Lebanon border.

alerts.title: US submarine USS Florida deployed to Persian Gulf amid escalation
alerts.summary: USS Florida (submarine) positioned at 26.33°N, 52.07°E in Persian Gulf, reportedly supporting strike operations. Concurrent with escalated US-Iran strikes and emergence of new Iranian coastal targets. Deployment reflects readiness for sustained operations in critical chokepoint zone.

alerts.title: UN suspends Strait of Hormuz shipping evacuation after vessel attack
alerts.summary: UN agency halted evacuation operations through Strait of Hormuz (26.8°N, 56.43°E) following confirmed attack on commercial cargo vessel. CENTCOM confirmed retaliatory US strikes on Iranian naval targets. Clustering of maritime incidents around Hormuz reflects sustained targeting of critical global energy chokepoint. Oil remains at $73/barrel; further escalation risks spike.

analysis_note: US-Iran strike cycle has definitively broken the fragile ceasefire framework; concurrent Hezbollah rejection of Israel-Lebanon deal and compressed nuclear breakout timeline (1-2 weeks) creates three simultaneous flashpoints with minimal diplomatic off-ramps as threat level holds at CRITICAL (9/10).
